question: Your aircraft weighs 1,437 lbs empty with a CG of 39.7". You weigh 195 lbs and will sit in the pilot's seat at 33", and you have 27 gallons of fuel in the tanks at 44". How many quarts of oil can you carry in the baggage compartment (130") without exceeding the aft CG limit of 42"?

You are practicing turns around a point, and while flying at 115 KTAS, you notice that it takes 46 seconds to complete a 360° turn. How far are you from the point?

Your cross country flight is 278 sm long on a true course of 172°. You will cruise at 173 KTAS while burning 12.3 GPH. Winds aloft are 220° at 28 knots. What is your minimum takeoff fuel to maintain a 30-minute VFR reserve?

The fuel truck pumps 220 gallons of JET-A (6.8 lbs/gal) per minute. Each of your wing tanks holds 14,000 lbs, and the center tank holds 10,500 lbs. If you landed with 6,000 lbs total, how long will it take to top off all the tanks?

You depart on your planned heading of 329° and ground speed of 109 KTS; your trip is 176 nm long. After 24 minutes, you notice that you are 2.7 sm left of course. What heading will take you directly to your destination?

You are instructed to hold east on the 090° radial at 31 km with right turns and standard legs. While abeam the holding fix on the outbound leg, you center a second CDI needle and determine you are now on the 081° radial at 31 km. With this information, what is the radius of your outbound turn?

How much quicker would it be to fly direct in a helicopter with a ground speed of 70 knots as opposed to driving at 99 km/h?

You are flying at FL320 and trying to navigate around thunderstorms. Your weather radar is tilted -2.5° and the bottom of the radar beam is painting the ground (4,000' MSL) as displayed on the 40 nm ring on the MFD. Your radar has a 7-degree beam width. You want to place the center of the radar beam at the freezing level (FL120) at 40 nm to get the best picture of reflectivity. What tilt angle should you set the radar?
You plot a course east from N47°57'14" W97°11'27" to N48°4'27" W96°11'8". What is the distance between these two points?

Airflow is the primary means of cooling an engine. The manufacturer has determined that decelerating from 100 KTS to 95 KTS, the engine is warmed by 7°F. However, for every 5-knot reduction in airspeed, the rate of temperature increase is reduced by 2.5%. Cruising at 90 knots the engine indicates 170°F. After slowing to 47 KTS, what is the new engine temperature?

After flying 113 nm of your 432 nm flight, you check your position and see that you have drifted 11.5° left of course. If you continue on your current track, how far from your destination will you be when you arrive?
What heading correction should you make to fly directly to the destination?

question: You put a fresh set of batteries into your headset giving it a capacity of 2,400 mAh. There is 432 nm remaining on your flight. True course is 114°. How much battery remains for your next flight?

question: Taking off from a small 2,652' grass strip, you rotate with 782' remaining. If you cross the end of the runway at 50' what is your climb gradient?
